question: Where is South Eastern University of Sri Lanka located?
ground_truth: SEUSL is located at University Park, Oluvil, #32360, Ampara District, Eastern Province, Sri Lanka. The permanent campus sits on 220 acres of land near the southeastern coastline, approximately 320 km from Colombo.

question: When was SEUSL established?
ground_truth: The South Eastern University College of Sri Lanka was established by gazette notification 88/9 of 26 July 1995. It was elevated to a fully-fledged National University on 27th March 1996 by gazette notification 916/7, and commenced full university activities from 15th May 1996.

question: What is the vision of SEUSL?
ground_truth: The vision of SEUSL is: "An Internationally Renowned Center in South Asia for Higher Learning and Innovations in Sciences, Technologies and Humanities."

question: Who is the Vice Chancellor of SEUSL?
ground_truth: The current Vice Chancellor of SEUSL is Prof. (Dr.) S. M. Junaideen. He can be reached at vcseu@seu.ac.lk or by phone at +94 67 2255138.

question: Who is the Chancellor of SEUSL?
ground_truth: The Chancellor of South Eastern University of Sri Lanka is Dr. Faisz Musthapha, PC.

question: How many faculties does SEUSL have?
ground_truth: SEUSL has 6 faculties: Faculty of Arts and Culture (FAC), Faculty of Management and Commerce (FMC), Faculty of Applied Sciences (FAS), Faculty of Islamic Studies and Arabic Language (FIA), Faculty of Engineering (FE), and Faculty of Technology (FT).

question: What is the general contact number of SEUSL?
ground_truth: The general contact numbers of SEUSL are +94 67 2255062, +94 67 2255063, and +94 67 2255064. The fax number is +94 67 2255217.

question: What is the email address of the Registrar's office?
ground_truth: The Registrar's office email is registrar@seu.ac.lk and the phone number is +94 67 2255168.

question: What is the official website of SEUSL?
ground_truth: The official website of SEUSL is https://www.seu.ac.lk/

question: Why was SEUSL established?
ground_truth: SEUSL was established to accommodate Muslim staff and students displaced from the University of Jaffna and later the Eastern University during civil unrest in the North and East of Sri Lanka.

question: What is the official website of SEUSL?
ground_truth: The official website of SEUSL is https://www.seu.ac.lk/

question: How many faculties does SEUSL have?
ground_truth: SEUSL has 6 faculties: Faculty of Arts and Culture (FAC), Faculty of Management and Commerce (FMC), Faculty of Applied Sciences (FAS), Faculty of Islamic Studies & Arabic Language (FIA), Faculty of Engineering (FE), and Faculty of Technology (FT).

question: With how many students did SEUSL begin?
ground_truth: The University College was ceremonially inaugurated with a batch of 33 students in October 1995.

question: What social media pages does SEUSL have?
ground_truth: SEUSL has a Facebook page (https://www.facebook.com/pages/South-Eastern-University-of-Sri-Lanka/992110304136392), Twitter (https://twitter.com/SEUSL_Oluvil), YouTube (https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCUtXxXZnUq3OxnpHmCc8heg), and LinkedIn (https://lk.linkedin.com/in/seusl).

question: How many students and academics does SEUSL currently have?
ground_truth: SEUSL has 240+ academics, 4450+ students, 6 faculties, and 25+ departments.

question: What are the original two faculties of SEUSL?
ground_truth: The University started with two Faculties — Faculty of Arts & Culture and Faculty of Management and Commerce — at Addalaichenai, sharing the premises of the Government Teachers' Training College.

ground_truth: The Chancellor of South Eastern University of Sri Lanka is Dr. Faisz Musthapha, PC.

question: Who was the founder Vice Chancellor of SEUSL?
ground_truth: The founder Vice Chancellor of SEUSL was Prof. MLA. Cader, who served from 1996.05.15 to 2003.05.11. He was also the Director of South Eastern University College from 1995.10.12 to 1996.05.14.

ground_truth: The Vice Chancellor before Prof. Junaideen was Prof. MMM. Najim, who served from 2015.06.22 to 2018.06.18 and again from 2018.08.10 to 2021.08.08.

question: What are the divisions under the Registrar's office?
ground_truth: The Registrar's office has divisions including: Administration, Academic Establishment, Non Academic Establishment, Examination, Staff & Students Welfare, Academic and Students Affairs, Capital Works & Planning, Legal and Documentations, Internal Audit, Works and Maintenance, Landscape, Security Services, and Students Accommodations.

ground_truth: The general contact numbers of SEUSL are +94 67 2255062, +94 67 2255063, and +94 67 2255064. The fax number is +94 67 2255217.

ground_truth: The Registrar's office email is registrar@seu.ac.lk and the phone number is +94 67 2255168.

ground_truth: The Vice Chancellor's email is vcseu@seu.ac.lk and the phone number is +94 67 2255138.

question: What is the address of the Registrar's office?
ground_truth: The Registrar's office address is: South Eastern University of Sri Lanka, P.O.Box - 01, University Park, Oluvil, #32280, Sri Lanka.

question: What departments are in the Faculty of Applied Sciences?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Applied Sciences (FAS) has 5 departments: Department of Biological Sciences, Department of Chemical Sciences, Department of Computer Science, Department of Mathematical Sciences, and Department of Physical Sciences.

question: What departments are in the Faculty of Arts and Culture?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Arts and Culture (FAC) has 8 departments: Department of Social Sciences, Department of Languages, Department of Geography, Department of Political Science, Department of Economics and Statistics, Department of English Language Teaching (DELT), Department of Sociology, and Department of Information Technology.

question: What departments are in the Faculty of Management and Commerce?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Management and Commerce (FMC) has 4 departments: Department of Accountancy and Finance, Department of Management, Department of Management and IT (MIT), and Department of Marketing Management.

question: What departments are in the Faculty of Technology?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Technology (FT) has 2 departments: Department of Biosystems Technology (BST) and Department of Information and Communication Technology (ICT).

question: What degree programs does the Faculty of Applied Sciences offer?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Applied Sciences offers B.Sc. in Biological Sciences, B.Sc. in Chemical Sciences, B.Sc. in Computer Science, B.Sc. in Mathematical Sciences, and B.Sc. in Physical Sciences. It also offers Diploma in Computer Technology (DCT) and Postgraduate Diploma in Applied Statistics (PGDAS).

question: What degree programs does the Faculty of Technology offer?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Technology offers B.Sc. in Biosystems Technology and B.Sc. in Information and Communication Technology (ICT). It also offers MPhil and PhD in Biosystems Technology by Research, and Short-Term Training Programmes by the ICT Department.

question: What degree programs does the Faculty of Arts and Culture offer?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Arts and Culture offers the Bachelor of Arts (BA) degree program. It also offers Diploma in English, Certificate in Human Rights, and Short Course in Geo-informatics.

question: What degree programs does the Faculty of Management and Commerce offer?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Management and Commerce offers undergraduate programs including Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA), Bachelor of Commerce (B.Com), and Bachelor of Science in Management and Information Technology. Postgraduate programs include Postgraduate Diploma (PGD), MPhil by Research, and PhD by Research.

question: What degree programs does the Faculty of Islamic Studies offer?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Islamic Studies and Arabic Language offers Bachelor of Arts in Islamic Studies and Bachelor of Arts in Arabic Language. It also offers Diploma programs in Islamic Studies and Arabic Language.

question: How can I apply to study at SEUSL?
ground_truth: Undergraduate admissions are handled by the University Grants Commission (UGC) of Sri Lanka. Students apply through UGC after receiving G.C.E. Advanced Level (A/L) results. For postgraduate programs, applications are submitted directly to the respective faculty's postgraduate unit. Contact the Registrar's office at registrar@seu.ac.lk or +94 67 2255168.

question: What qualifications do I need to enter SEUSL?
ground_truth: Admission to undergraduate programs at SEUSL is through the University Grants Commission (UGC) based on results from the G.C.E. Advanced Level (A/L) examination. Students are admitted based on their A/L results, Z-score, district quotas, and the UGC's national admissions policy.

question: What is the medium of instruction at SEUSL?
ground_truth: The medium of instruction varies by faculty. English is used in the Faculty of Applied Sciences, Faculty of Engineering, and Faculty of Technology. Tamil and English are used in the Faculty of Arts and Culture and Faculty of Management and Commerce. Arabic is used in the Faculty of Islamic Studies and Arabic Language.

question: Does SEUSL have a library?
ground_truth: Yes, SEUSL has a main university library (Dr. A.M.A. Azeez Library) located at the center of campus, and a dedicated Science Library. The main library website is http://www.seu.ac.lk/library/. Opening hours are Monday to Friday 8:00 AM–8:00 PM and Saturday 8:00 AM–5:00 PM.

question: What is the student web portal?
ground_truth: Students can access the student web portal at http://st.seu.ac.lk/ and the Virtual Learning Environment (VLE) at http://vle.seu.ac.lk/

question: What is the Career Guidance Unit?
ground_truth: The Career Guidance Unit helps students with career planning, job placement, internships, and professional development. Established in 1998, it is directed by Dr. J.A. Thamboo. It assists with CV writing, interview preparation, career fairs, and company presentations. Website: http://www.seu.ac.lk/careerguidanceunit/index.php

question: What is the ICT Centre at SEUSL?
ground_truth: The ICT Centre manages the IT infrastructure of the university and provides IT-related facilities and services to staff and students, including SEU email, web applications, internet, WiFi, IT security, software, and IT labs. Website: http://www.seu.ac.lk/ictcentre/index.php

question: Does SEUSL offer postgraduate programs?
ground_truth: Yes, SEUSL offers postgraduate programs including MPhil (Master of Philosophy) by Research, PhD (Doctor of Philosophy) by Research, and Postgraduate Diploma programs. These are offered through the Postgraduate Units of the respective faculties (FMC, FAC, FAS, FT).

question: What is the Virtual Learning Environment (VLE)?
ground_truth: SEUSL operates a Virtual Learning Environment (VLE) for online course delivery, assignments, and learning materials. The main VLE is at http://vle.seu.ac.lk/, FAS VLE at https://fasvle.seu.ac.lk/, and FT VLE at http://vle.seu.ac.lk/course/index.php?categoryid=137

question: Who is the Head of the ICT Department in the Faculty of Technology?
ground_truth: The Head of the Department of Information and Communication Technology (ICT) in the Faculty of Technology is Mr. RK. Ahmadh Rifai Kariapper, Senior Lecturer in ICT.

question: Who is the Head of the BST Department?
ground_truth: The Head of the Department of Biosystems Technology (BST) in the Faculty of Technology is Dr. Muneeb M. Musthafa, Senior Lecturer in BST.

question: What is the English Language Teaching Unit (ELTU)?
ground_truth: The ELTU supports students in improving English language skills critical for academic success. It runs language improvement programs and workshops and is especially useful for students in faculties where English is the medium of instruction. Website: http://www.seu.ac.lk/eltu/index.php

question: What is OSPIM?
ground_truth: OSPIM is the Office of Strategic Planning and Implementation Monitoring (also called Office of Strategic Planning and International Matters). It handles strategic planning, action plans, progress monitoring, international student admissions, MoU partnerships, and study abroad programs. Director: Dr. A. Ilmudeen. Email: ospim@seu.ac.lk. Website: http://www.seu.ac.lk/ospim/index.php

question: Does SEUSL accept foreign students?
ground_truth: Yes, SEUSL accepts foreign students. They can contact OSPIM (Office of Strategic Planning and International Matters) for information at http://www.seu.ac.lk/ospim/index.php

question: What is the Research and Innovation Centre (RIC)?
ground_truth: The Research and Innovation Centre (RIC) was established in 2014. Its objective is to promote high-quality basic and applied research and development activities including collaborative and interdisciplinary research. Director: Prof. (Dr.) Abdul Majeed Muzathik. Email: directorric@seu.ac.lk, Phone: +94 67 2052805.

question: When was the Faculty of Islamic Studies established?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Islamic Studies and Arabic Language (FIA) was established in October 2005 with the first batch of 50 students for Academic Year 2005/2006. It is the ONLY faculty of this nature in any university in Sri Lanka.
question: What departments are in the Faculty of Islamic Studies and Arabic Language?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Islamic Studies and Arabic Language (FIA) has 3 departments: Department of Islamic Studies, Department of Arabic Language, and Interdisciplinary Course Unit.

question: When was the Faculty of Applied Sciences established?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Applied Sciences was established in 1997 at Sammanthurai.

question: When was the Faculty of Islamic Studies and Arabic Language established?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Islamic Studies and Arabic Language (FIA) was established in October 2005 with the first batch of 50 students for the Academic Year 2005/2006.

question: What is unique about the Faculty of Islamic Studies and Arabic Language?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Islamic Studies and Arabic Language (FIA) is the ONLY faculty of this nature in any university in Sri Lanka.

question: What are the original faculties of SEUSL?
ground_truth: The university started with two faculties — Faculty of Arts & Culture and Faculty of Management and Commerce — at Addalaichenai, sharing the premises of the Government Teachers' Training College. They moved to the permanent campus at Oluvil in May 1998.

question: What is an End Semester Examination (ESE)?
What is the mission of the Faculty of Management and Commerce?
ground_truth: The FMC mission is to provide a quality education in business studies which blends the best of relevant theory and practice with an innovative approach to teaching, learning and research giving due consideration to the changing environment, the real demands of business world, and the contemporary development needs of the South Eastern Region and the country as a whole.

question: What is the mission of the Faculty of Arts and Culture?
ground_truth: The FAC mission is to produce high quality graduates with analytical mind, research capabilities, leadership quality, managerial skills and ethical and moral values by creating a conducive environment to adopt innovative teaching, learning and research to meet the fast changing needs of the society with dedication and commitment of competent academic and supportive staff.

question: What is the vision of the Faculty of Islamic Studies and Arabic Language?
ground_truth: The vision of FIA is to be a leading centre for the integration of Islamic Studies and Arabic Language.

question: What degree programs does the Faculty of Applied Sciences offer?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Applied Sciences offers B.Sc. in Biological Sciences, B.Sc. in Chemical Sciences, B.Sc. in Computer Science, B.Sc. in Mathematical Sciences, and B.Sc. in Physical Sciences. It also offers Diploma in Computer Technology (DCT), Postgraduate Diploma in Applied Statistics (PGDAS), MPhil and PhD by Research.

question: What degree programs does the Faculty of Technology offer?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Technology offers B.Sc. in Biosystems Technology and B.Sc. in Information and Communication Technology (ICT). It also offers MPhil in Biosystems Technology by Research, PhD in Biosystems Technology by Research, and Short-Term Training Programmes by the ICT Department.

question: What degree programs does the Faculty of Arts and Culture offer?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Arts and Culture offers the Bachelor of Arts (BA) degree program. It also offers Diploma in English, Certificate in Human Rights, and Short Course in Geo-informatics.

question: What degree programs does the Faculty of Management and Commerce offer?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Management and Commerce offers Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA), Bachelor of Commerce (B.Com), and Bachelor of Science in Management and Information Technology. Postgraduate programs include PGD, MPhil by Research, and PhD by Research.

question: What degree programs does the Faculty of Islamic Studies and Arabic Language offer?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Islamic Studies and Arabic Language offers Bachelor of Arts in Islamic Studies and Bachelor of Arts in Arabic Language. It also offers Diploma in Islamic Studies and Diploma in Arabic Language.

question: Does SEUSL offer postgraduate programs?
ground_truth: Yes, SEUSL offers postgraduate programs including MPhil (Master of Philosophy) by Research, PhD (Doctor of Philosophy) by Research, and Postgraduate Diploma programs through the Postgraduate Units of the respective faculties (FMC, FAC, FAS, FT).

question: What is the Diploma in Computer Technology (DCT)?
ground_truth: The Diploma in Computer Technology (DCT) is a diploma program offered by the Faculty of Applied Sciences (FAS). Applications for the 2026 intake are open, and the application form is available at https://www.seu.ac.lk/fas/assets/img/DCT/Application2026.doc.

question: What is the Postgraduate Diploma in Applied Statistics?
ground_truth: The Postgraduate Diploma in Applied Statistics (PGDAS) is a postgraduate program offered by the Faculty of Applied Sciences (FAS). The 2026 intake is open with the application deadline extended to 28/02/2026. Application form: https://www.seu.ac.lk/fas/assets/img/PGDIP/PGDASApplication2026.doc.

question: What is the B.Sc. in Information and Communication Technology about?
ground_truth: The B.Sc. in ICT offered by the Faculty of Technology covers software development, networking, database management, cybersecurity, and multimedia. The Department of ICT also offers Short-Term Training Programmes (STTP) for external candidates.

question: What is the B.Sc. in Biosystems Technology about?
ground_truth: The B.Sc. in Biosystems Technology offered by the Faculty of Technology covers agricultural technology, bioprocessing, environmental technology, and food technology. The department also offers MPhil and PhD in Biosystems Technology by Research.

For postgraduate programs, applications are submitted directly to the respective faculty's postgraduate unit. Contact the Registrar's office at registrar@seu.ac.lk or +94 67 2255168.

question: What qualifications are needed to enter SEUSL?
ground_truth: Admission to undergraduate programs at SEUSL is through the University Grants Commission (UGC) based on results from the G.C.E. Advanced Level (A/L) examination. Students are admitted based on their A/L results and the UGC's national admissions policy.

question: Does SEUSL accept foreign students?
ground_truth: Yes, SEUSL accepts foreign students. Contact the Office of Strategic Planning and International Matters (OSPIM) for information: http://www.seu.ac.lk/ospim/index.php.

question: What is the medium of instruction at SEUSL?
ground_truth: The medium of instruction varies by faculty. English is used in the Faculty of Applied Sciences, Faculty of Engineering, and Faculty of Technology. Tamil and English are used in the Faculty of Arts and Culture and Faculty of Management and Commerce. Arabic is used in the Faculty of Islamic Studies and Arabic Language.

question: What is End Semester Examination (ESE)?
ground_truth: End Semester Examinations are formal written examinations held at the end of each semester. They form a major component of student assessment for each course unit.

question: What is Continuous Assessment (CA)?
ground_truth: Continuous Assessment (CA) refers to ongoing evaluation throughout the semester through assignments, practicals, quizzes, mid-semester tests, presentations, and other in-course activities. CA results are combined with ESE results to determine final grades.

question: Does SEUSL offer diploma programs?
ground_truth: Yes, SEUSL offers various diploma programs including Diploma in English (FAC), Diploma in Computer Technology/DCT (FAS), Postgraduate Diploma in Applied Statistics (FAS), Diploma programs in Islamic Studies and Arabic Language (FIA), and Short-Term Training Programmes in ICT (FT).

question: What welfare facilities are available for students?
ground_truth: SEUSL provides Student Support Services and Welfare (SSSW), Career Guidance Unit, Physical Education Unit, Library, ICT Centre, Virtual Learning Environment (VLE), English Language Teaching Unit (ELTU), hostel/boarding facilities, health centre, subsidized canteen, and various student clubs and societies.

question: Who was the founder Vice Chancellor of SEUSL?
ground_truth: Prof. MLA. Cader was the Founder Vice-Chancellor, serving from 15th May 1996 to 11th May 2003. He was also Director of South Eastern University College from 12th October 1995 to 14th May 1996.

question: What is the Vice Chancellor's email?

ground_truth: SEUSL has official pages on Facebook (https://www.facebook.com/pages/South-Eastern-University-of-Sri-Lanka/992110304136392), Twitter (@SEUSL_Oluvil), YouTube, and LinkedIn.

question: What is the Diploma in Computer Technology (DCT)?
ground_truth: The Diploma in Computer Technology (DCT) is a diploma program offered by the Faculty of Applied Sciences. The 2026 intake is open with applications available at the FAS website: https://www.seu.ac.lk/fas/assets/img/DCT/Application2026.doc

question: What research journals does the Faculty of Applied Sciences publish?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Applied Sciences publishes the Journal of Science (JSC) available at http://www.seu.ac.lk/jsc/. It also hosts the Annual Science Research Session (ASRS) and the First International Conference on AI & Data Science (ICAIDS 2025).

question: What research journals does the Faculty of Arts and Culture publish?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Arts and Culture publishes KALAM International Research Journal, Pravaham Journal, and Journal of Social Review (JSR). It also holds an Annual Research Conference.

question: What is the mission of the Faculty of Management and Commerce?
ground_truth: The mission of FMC is to provide a quality education in business studies which blends the best of relevant theory and practice with an innovative approach to teaching, learning and research giving due consideration to the changing environment, the real demands of business world, and the contemporary development needs of the South Eastern Region and the country as a whole.

question: What MoU did SEUSL sign with the Colombo Stock Exchange?
ground_truth: South Eastern University and the Colombo Stock Exchange (CSE) signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) on 16 August 2024 to foster collaboration in education, research, and professional development. The MoU covers joint research projects, internships, guest lectures, and training programs.

question: What is the Industrial Training Cell (ITC)?
ground_truth: The Industrial Training Cell (ITC) of the Faculty of Applied Sciences was established in 2018. Its prime objective is to produce science graduates skilled in industrial activities by exposing them to actual working environments. It organizes industrial training by linking students with relevant industries.

question: What is the Centre for Soil and Water Research (CSWR)?
ground_truth: The Centre for Soil and Water Research (CSWR) is the first centre established at FAS in 2004, with external NGO support by Mr. MF Nawas and Mr. EMJM Rizvi. It is located at the Department of Chemical Sciences. After the Tsunami, CSWR was actively involved in community services analyzing affected water wells.

question: What is the Student Support Services and Welfare (SSSW)?
ground_truth: SSSW coordinates student support services and welfare by networking the Student Services and Registration Division, Health Centre, Physical Education Division, Maintenance Division, Security Division, Marshal Unit, Student Counseling System, and Career Guidance Unit. Director: Dr. ALM. Ayoobkhan. Email: directorsssw@seu.ac.lk.

question: How many students does SEUSL have?
ground_truth: SEUSL has over 4,450 students according to the university's official statistics.

question: How many academic staff does SEUSL have?
ground_truth: SEUSL has over 240 academics according to the university's official statistics.

question: How many departments does SEUSL have?
ground_truth: SEUSL has over 25 departments across its 6 faculties.

question: What is the library's vision?
ground_truth: The SEUSL Library's vision is: "To be a knowledge centre enabling intense intellectual inquiry by providing a dynamic learning environment and proactively linking the users with global knowledge to support the vision of SEUSL as that of a world class university."

question: Does SEUSL have hostel facilities?
ground_truth: Yes, SEUSL provides on-campus hostel accommodation for both male and female students. Spaces are limited; priority is given to students who live far from the university, students from low-income families, and first-year students.

question: What is the Postgraduate Diploma in Applied Statistics?
ground_truth: The Postgraduate Diploma in Applied Statistics (PGDAS) is a postgraduate program offered by the Faculty of Applied Sciences. The 2026 intake is open with applications available at the FAS website: https://www.seu.ac.lk/fas/assets/img/PGDIP/PGDASApplication2026.doc

question: What divisions are under the Registrar's office?
ground_truth: The Registrar's office oversees: Administration Division, Academic Establishment Division, Non Academic Establishment Division, Examination Division, Staff & Students Welfare Division, Academic and Students Affairs Division, Capital Works & Planning Division, Legal and Documentations, Internal Audit Division, Works and Maintenance Division, Landscape Division, Security Services Division, and Students Accommodations.

question: What is the address of the Registrar's office?
ground_truth: The Registrar's office address is South Eastern University of Sri Lanka, P.O. Box 01, University Park, Oluvil, #32280, Sri Lanka. Phone: +94 67 2255168, Fax: +94 67 2255190, Email: registrar@seu.ac.lk.

question: Do I need to be fluent in English to study at SEUSL?
ground_truth: A good working knowledge of English is helpful, especially for faculties where English is the medium of instruction (FAS, FE, FT). SEUSL has an English Language Teaching Unit (ELTU) to support students with English language skills.

question: What is the GPA system used at SEUSL?
ground_truth: SEUSL uses a 4.0 GPA scale. Grade boundaries: A+/A = 4.0, A- = 3.7, B+ = 3.3, B = 3.0, B- = 2.7, C+ = 2.3, C = 2.0, C- = 1.7, D+ = 1.3, D = 1.0, E (Fail) = 0.0. Cumulative GPA (CGPA) is calculated at the end of each semester across all courses.
question: What is the course structure at SEUSL?
ground_truth: Degree programmes at SEUSL are structured on a credit-based system with semesters. Students must complete a required number of credits through a combination of core courses, electives, and practical work to be eligible for their degree.

question: Do I need to be fluent in English to study at SEUSL?
ground_truth: A good working knowledge of English is helpful, especially for faculties where English is the medium of instruction (FAS, FE, FT). SEUSL has an English Language Teaching Unit (ELTU) to support students with English language skills.

question: Does SEUSL have a library?
ground_truth: Yes, SEUSL has a main university library (Dr. A.M.A. Azeez Library) and a dedicated Science Library. The main library website is http://www.seu.ac.lk/library/ and the Science Library is at http://www.seu.ac.lk/library/science_library/. The library provides physical and digital resources for students and researchers.

question: What are the library opening hours?
ground_truth: The library opening hours are Monday to Friday 8:00 AM to 8:00 PM, Saturday 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. The library is closed on Sundays and public holidays.

question: What e-book resources does the SEUSL library provide?
ground_truth: The library provides e-books from subscribed Taylor & Francis eBooks (accessible within the SEUSL network), Shibboleth/Remote Access for off-campus access, Library Catalog search, E-Repository (SEUIR) at http://ir.lib.seu.ac.lk/, and E-Databases.

question: What is the student web portal?
ground_truth: Students can access the student web portal at http://st.seu.ac.lk/ and the Virtual Learning Environment (VLE) at http://vle.seu.ac.lk/.

question: What is the Virtual Learning Environment (VLE)?
ground_truth: SEUSL operates a Virtual Learning Environment (VLE) for online course delivery, assignments, and learning materials. The main VLE is at http://vle.seu.ac.lk/, FAS VLE at https://fasvle.seu.ac.lk/, and FT VLE at http://vle.seu.ac.lk/course/index.php?categoryid=137.

question: What is the ICT Centre at SEUSL?
ground_truth: The ICT Centre manages the IT infrastructure of the University and provides IT related facilities and services to staff and students, including SEU email (@seu.ac.lk), web applications, internet connectivity, Wi-Fi coverage across campus, IT security, licensed software, IT labs, and technical support. Website: http://www.seu.ac.lk/ictcentre/index.php.

question: What is the Career Guidance Unit?
ground_truth: The Career Guidance Unit was established in 1998 at SEUSL. Director: Dr. J.A. Thamboo (Email: jathamboo@seu.ac.lk, Phone: +94 67 2255065). It assists students with career planning, job placement, industrial training, internship opportunities, professional development workshops, and CV writing. Website: http://www.seu.ac.lk/careerguidanceunit/index.php.

question: What is the English Language Teaching Unit (ELTU)?
ground_truth: The ELTU supports students in improving English language skills critical for academic success. Website: http://www.seu.ac.lk/eltu/index.php.

question: What is OSPIM?
ground_truth: OSPIM is the Office of Strategic Planning and International Matters. It is the strategic planning and monitoring wing of the University. It handles international student admissions, partnerships, MoU agreements, and strategic planning. Website: http://www.seu.ac.lk/ospim/index.php.

question: What is the Research and Innovation Centre (RIC)?
ground_truth: The Research and Innovation Centre (RIC) was established in 2014. It promotes and facilitates high-quality basic and applied research, collaborative and interdisciplinary research at university level. Website: http://www.seu.ac.lk/ric/index.php.

question: What GPA do I need to graduate with First Class honors?
ground_truth: First Class honors requires a CGPA of 3.7 or above. Second Class Upper requires CGPA 3.3+, Second Class Lower requires CGPA 2.7+. A minimum CGPA of 2.0 is required to progress to the next year.

question: What is the minimum attendance required to sit for exams?
ground_truth: A minimum of 80% attendance is required for each course to be eligible to sit the final examination. Students below 80% attendance receive a 'not eligible' notation and must repeat the course.

question: What happens if I miss an exam due to illness?
ground_truth: Submit a medical certificate from a government hospital to the Registrar/Examinations Division within 5 working days. Apply for a medical repeat examination. Approval is at the discretion of the Faculty Examination Board. Private clinic certificates may not be accepted.

question: What is academic probation at SEUSL?
ground_truth: Academic probation is issued when a student's CGPA falls below the minimum threshold (usually 2.0) at the end of a semester. If the CGPA remains below the threshold for two consecutive semesters, the student may be required to withdraw from the program.

question: What facilities are inside the university hostels?
ground_truth: Basic hostel rooms have a bed, study table, chair, and wardrobe/shelf space. Common facilities include shared bathrooms, toilets, a common room/TV room, and a study hall. Students should bring their own bedding, pillow, towel, and study materials.

question: What are the hostel curfew timings?
ground_truth: Curfew for female hostels is usually 10 PM. Other rules include no unauthorized visitors in rooms, no cooking inside rooms, no loud music after 10 PM, and maintaining cleanliness of common areas.

question: Is university education free at SEUSL?
ground_truth: Yes, Sri Lanka provides free university education to students admitted through the UGC system — no tuition fees for undergraduate degrees. However, small administrative fees apply: registration fee, examination fees per semester, library fees, and hostel fees.

question: What is the Mahapola scholarship?
ground_truth: The Mahapola Higher Education Scholarship provides a monthly allowance of approximately LKR 5,000–7,500 to eligible undergraduates at state universities. Eligibility is based on academic merit and financial need. Apply through your faculty's student affairs office in the first month of registration.

question: What is the Bursary scholarship?
ground_truth: The Bursary is a needs-based financial grant from the university for students with proven financial hardship. It is separate from Mahapola and provides additional funds for living expenses and books. Apply with an income declaration signed by your Grama Niladhari (GN officer).

question: What is the typical cost of living per month in Oluvil?
ground_truth: Estimated monthly budget: Accommodation LKR 3,000–8,000 (off-campus), food LKR 6,000–10,000, transport LKR 1,000–2,000, stationery/books LKR 500–2,000, personal LKR 1,000–3,000. Total approximately LKR 12,000–25,000/month. Oluvil is one of the most affordable university towns in Sri Lanka.

question: Does SEUSL have a medical clinic?
ground_truth: Yes, SEUSL has a university health centre staffed by a medical officer who holds regular clinical sessions. For minor ailments, medicines are provided free of charge to students. Serious conditions are referred to Kalmunai Base Hospital (approximately 5 km away).

question: Is there a university canteen at SEUSL?
ground_truth: Yes. The main university canteen serves breakfast, lunch, and dinner on weekdays. Food is subsidized for students. Small student-run kiosks and food stalls also operate near hostel areas and faculty buildings. The canteen serves halal Sri Lankan meals — rice and curry, short eats, and beverages.

question: What sports facilities does SEUSL have?
ground_truth: SEUSL has a cricket ground, football/soccer pitch, volleyball and netball courts, badminton courts, and a multi-purpose indoor sports hall. These are maintained by the Physical Education department. Inter-faculty tournaments and SLUG (Sri Lanka University Games) are held using these facilities.

question: Are there ATMs or banks near the campus?
ground_truth: There are no ATMs directly on campus. The nearest bank branches and ATMs are in Kalmunai town (approximately 5 km from campus). Bank of Ceylon, People's Bank, and Commercial Bank have branches there.

question: What student clubs and societies exist at SEUSL?
ground_truth: SEUSL has numerous clubs including Computer Society, Science Society, Management Society, Debating Club, Drama Society, Music Circle, Photography Club, Leo Club (community service), Muslim Students Association, Environment Club, and various faculty-specific societies.

question: What is Freshers' Night at SEUSL?
ground_truth: Freshers' Night is the main celebratory event welcoming first-year students, organized by second-year students a few weeks after orientation. It features cultural performances, music, welcome speeches, crowning of Freshers' King/Queen, and student skits.

question: What is SLUG and does SEUSL participate?
ground_truth: SLUG stands for Sri Lanka University Games — the national inter-university sports championship. SEUSL participates and top-performing athletes are selected to represent SEUSL. SLUG includes cricket, football, volleyball, athletics, swimming, and other sports.

question: When does industrial training take place?
ground_truth: Industrial training typically takes place in Year 3 or between Year 3 and Year 4. Duration is usually 6 months to 1 year. The university provides a list of approved companies and students can also find their own placement (subject to approval). Start searching at least 3–4 months before the IT period begins.

question: Who was the Vice Chancellor before Prof. Junaideen?
ground_truth: Prof. MMM. Najim served as Vice-Chancellor immediately before Prof. Junaideen, from 2018.08.10 to 2021.08.08 (his second term). Between his two terms, Prof. UMA Coomaraswamy served as Competent Authority from 2018.06.19 to 2018.08.09.

question: What e-book resources does the library provide?
ground_truth: The library provides subscribed Taylor & Francis eBooks accessible within the SEUSL network or via Shibboleth/Remote Access for off-campus use. It also provides e-databases, an e-repository (SEUIR), and online journals through the UGC E-Journals Consortium.
question: What is the Student Support Services and Welfare (SSSW)?
ground_truth: The SSSW coordinates student support services and student welfare by networking the Student Services and Registration Division, Service Divisions (Health Centre, Physical Education, Maintenance, Security, Marshal Unit), Student Counseling System, and Career Guidance Unit. Director: Dr. ALM. Ayoobkhan. Email: directorsssw@seu.ac.lk, Phone: +94 67 2255389.

question: Does SEUSL have hostel facilities?
ground_truth: Yes, SEUSL has hostel facilities for students. The university has Sub-Warden posts for both male and female student hostels. For accommodation information, contact the university administration or welfare office.

question: What is the Internal Quality Assurance Unit (IQAU)?
ground_truth: The Internal Quality Assurance Unit (IQAU) oversees quality standards and accreditation processes at SEUSL. Website: http://www.seu.ac.lk/iqau/.

question: What is CEDPL?
ground_truth: CEDPL is the Centre for Extension and Distance and Professional Learning. It offers extension education programs and distance learning. Website: http://www.seu.ac.lk/cedpl/index.php.

question: What is TISC?
ground_truth: TISC is the Technology and Innovation Support Centre. It supports technology transfer and intellectual property. Website: http://www.seu.ac.lk/tisc/index.php.

question: What is the UBL Cell?
ground_truth: The UBL Cell (University Business Linkage Cell) facilitates collaboration between the university and industry. Website: http://www.seu.ac.lk/ubl/index.php.

question: What is the Staff Development Centre (SDC)?
ground_truth: The Staff Development Centre (SDC) handles professional development for academic and non-academic staff at SEUSL. Website: http://www.seu.ac.lk/sdc/index.php.

question: What is the Science Research Centre (SRCFAS)?
ground_truth: The Science Research Centre (SRCFAS) is a centre in the Faculty of Applied Sciences that conducts research in biotechnology, environmental science, materials science, physics, and chemistry.

question: What is the Industrial Training Cell (ITC)?
ground_truth: The Industrial Training Cell (ITC) was established in 2018 in the Faculty of Applied Sciences. It places students in industries for practical training experience.

question: What is the Centre for Soil and Water Research (CSWR)?
ground_truth: The Centre for Soil and Water Research (CSWR) was established in 2004 by Mr. MF Nawas and Mr. EMJM Rizvi in the Faculty of Applied Sciences.

question: What welfare facilities are available for students?
ground_truth: SEUSL provides: Student Support Services and Welfare (SSSW), Career Guidance Unit, Physical Education Unit, Library (main and science library), ICT Centre, Virtual Learning Environment (VLE), English Language Teaching Unit (ELTU), hostel accommodation, and University Life activities and clubs.

question: What research journals does the Faculty of Applied Sciences publish?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Applied Sciences publishes the Journal of Science (JSC) at http://www.seu.ac.lk/jsc/. It also hosts the Annual Science Research Session (ASRS) and the First International Conference on AI & Data Science (ICAIDS 2025).

question: What research journals does the Faculty of Arts and Culture publish?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Arts and Culture publishes the KALAM International Research Journal, the Pravaham Journal, and the Journal of Social Review (JSR). It also hosts an Annual Research Conference.

question: What conferences has SEUSL hosted recently?
ground_truth: SEUSL recently hosted the First International Conference on AI & Data Science (ICAIDS 2025) organized by FAS, the Annual Science Research Session (ASRS 2025) by FAS, the 11th International Symposium (INTSYMFIA 2026) by FIA, and annual international research conferences hosted by FMC.

question: What MoU did SEUSL sign recently?
ground_truth: SEUSL (Faculty of Management and Commerce) signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the Colombo Stock Exchange (CSE) on 16 August 2024 to foster collaboration in education, research, and professional development.

question: What is the SEUSL Institutional Repository?
ground_truth: The SEUSL Institutional Repository (SEUIR) was established in 2006 and is accessible at http://ir.lib.seu.ac.lk/. It collects and provides open access to institutional research outputs including theses, conference proceedings, journal articles, and books authored by SEUSL academics.

question: What types of content are accepted in the SEUSL Institutional Repository?
ground_truth: The SEUIR accepts: conference proceedings of SEUSL, thesis and dissertations, journals published by SEUSL, articles authored by SEUSL academics (with copyright clearance), books and book chapters (non-textbooks, case-by-case), datasets, and grey literature such as technical reports and working papers.

question: What guidelines and regulations are available for download from SEUSL?
ground_truth: Available guidelines include: Guidelines to Supervisors and Invigilators, Regulations Related to Maintain Attendance and Calculation of Percentage Attendance, Regulations Related to Setting, Printing and Evaluation of Examinations, Guideline for Field Trips, Terms of Reference of the Grievance Hearing Committee, and Grievance Application Form. Download page: https://www.seu.ac.lk/guidelines.php.

question: What forms are available for download from SEUSL?
ground_truth: Downloadable forms from https://www.seu.ac.lk/download.php include: Leave Application Form, Prior Approval Form (OR/OT/01), Official Email Request Form, Application Form (Agrahara Medical Scheme), Guest House Booking Form (GA/G/01), APC Booking Form (GA/G/02), Application for Railway Warrant (GA/G/04), Gate Pass for Returnable Items (GA/G/05), PRN Forms for Capital and Consumable Items, ERSU Repair Request form, and Works Order (SEU/WMD/01).

question: How does a staff member request an official SEUSL email?
ground_truth: Staff and students can request an official @seu.ac.lk email by submitting the Official Email Request Form available at https://www.seu.ac.lk/download.php. Students must include their Student Registration Number (e.g., xxxeg23.001@seu.ac.lk).

question: What is the Internal Quality Assurance Unit (IQAU)?
ground_truth: The Internal Quality Assurance Unit (IQAU) oversees quality standards and accreditation processes at SEUSL. Website: http://www.seu.ac.lk/iqau/

question: What is the Staff Development Centre (SDC)?
ground_truth: The Staff Development Centre (SDC) handles professional development for academic and non-academic staff at SEUSL. Website: http://www.seu.ac.lk/sdc/index.php

question: What is CEDPL?
ground_truth: CEDPL is the Centre for Extension and Distance and Professional Learning. It offers extension education programs and distance learning at SEUSL. Website: http://www.seu.ac.lk/cedpl/index.php

question: What is TISC at SEUSL?
ground_truth: TISC is the Technology and Innovation Support Centre. It supports technology transfer and intellectual property at SEUSL. Website: http://www.seu.ac.lk/tisc/index.php

question: What is the UBL Cell?
ground_truth: The University Business Linkage Cell (UBL Cell) facilitates collaboration between the university and industry. Website: http://www.seu.ac.lk/ubl/index.php

question: How many students started with SEUSL when it was first inaugurated?
ground_truth: The University College was ceremonially inaugurated with a batch of 33 students in October 1995.

question: What is SERLIN?
ground_truth: SERLIN stands for South Eastern Regional Library & Information Network of Sri Lanka. It is associated with the SEUSL Library.

question: Does SEUSL have a plagiarism detection tool?
ground_truth: Yes, SEUSL has Turnitin available as a plagiarism detection tool through the university library.

question: What is the assessment split between exams and coursework?
ground_truth: A typical split is 40% coursework (assignments, mid-semester tests, practicals, presentations) and 60% final examination. Some courses are 100% coursework or 100% exam — check the course outline distributed at the start of each semester.

question: What is the policy on plagiarism and academic dishonesty?
ground_truth: SEUSL has a strict academic integrity policy. Plagiarism can result in a zero mark for that assessment, course failure, or suspension. All written assignments are subject to plagiarism checks. Students must use proper citation and referencing in all academic work.

question: How do I request my official academic transcript?
ground_truth: Submit a written request to the Registrar's office with your student index number, full name, degree program, and purpose of transcript. Transcripts are usually ready within 5–10 working days. A fee is charged per copy. Official transcripts are sealed and stamped.

question: Can I defer or suspend my studies at SEUSL?
ground_truth: Yes. Submit a formal written application to the Registrar via your HoD with valid reasons and supporting documents. Medical deferral requires a certified government hospital letter. A maximum of one academic year deferment is usually allowed during the full degree program.

question: What documents do I need on registration day?
ground_truth: Bring originals and photocopies of: National Identity Card (NIC), A/L exam results certificate, O/L exam results certificate, birth certificate, medical fitness certificate (from a government hospital), police clearance certificate, 6–8 passport-size photographs, school leaving certificate, and the UGC admission letter.

question: What happens during freshers' orientation week?
ground_truth: Orientation week activities include: campus and faculty tour, Dean and HoD welcome address, course registration, hostel room assignment, anti-ragging briefing (mandatory by UGC law), student union and society introductions, and senior student mentoring sessions. It is compulsory to attend all orientation activities.

question: What is the minimum CGPA required to progress to the next year?
ground_truth: A minimum CGPA of 2.0 is required to progress to the next year of study.

question: How many credits are needed to graduate from SEUSL?
ground_truth: You need to complete a minimum of 120–130 credits to graduate. Each course carries 2–4 credits. Students typically take 5–7 courses per semester amounting to 15–21 credits.

question: Is there an alumni network at SEUSL?
ground_truth: Yes. SEUSL has an active alumni association. Once graduated, you can join for networking, job referrals, and mentoring juniors. Connecting with alumni on LinkedIn during studies can open doors to internships and job opportunities.

question: Is Oluvil safe for students?
ground_truth: Yes. Oluvil is a small, quiet coastal community and the university campus is its main landmark. The area is generally very safe for students. The town has improved significantly in infrastructure since the post-war development period.

question: I want to study computer science. Which faculty and what is the program?
ground_truth: Computer Science is offered by the Faculty of Applied Sciences (FAS) as a B.Sc. in Computer Science (4-year program). It covers programming, algorithms, data structures, AI, and networks. The department head is Mr. AL Hanees (alhanees@seu.ac.lk). FAS is located at Sammanthurai. The medium of instruction is English.

question: I'm interested in IT but also want a business component. What program should I consider?
ground_truth: You could consider the B.Sc. in Information and Communication Technology (ICT) from the Faculty of Technology, or the Bachelor of Science in Management and Information Technology from the Faculty of Management and Commerce (FMC). The FMC option blends IT with business, management information systems, and e-business.

question: Who do I contact if I have a problem with my exam results?
ground_truth: Contact the Examination Division under the Registrar's office. The Registrar's office can be reached at registrar@seu.ac.lk or +94 67 2255168. The Examination Division is accessible at http://www.seu.ac.lk/generaladmin/exams/

question: How do I get my university email address?
ground_truth: Request a university email using the Official Email Request Form available at https://www.seu.ac.lk/download.php. Students must include their Student Registration Number in the format xxxeg23.001@seu.ac.lk. The ICT Centre manages email accounts.

question: Which faculty should I contact if I want to study Arabic?
ground_truth: You should contact the Faculty of Islamic Studies and Arabic Language (FIA). They offer a Bachelor of Arts in Arabic Language and a Diploma in Arabic Language. Dean: Ash Sheikh. MHA. Munas. Email: munas@seu.ac.lk. Phone: +94 67 2255127.

question: What is the difference between FT ICT and FAS Computer Science?
ground_truth: Both are IT-related programs but differ in focus. FAS B.Sc. in Computer Science (Faculty of Applied Sciences) emphasizes theoretical foundations — programming, algorithms, data structures, AI, and networks. FT B.Sc. in ICT (Faculty of Technology) focuses on applied and practical skills — software development, networking, database management, cybersecurity, and multimedia. FAS is at Sammanthurai while FT is at the main Oluvil campus.

question: Can I do a PhD at SEUSL?
ground_truth: Yes. SEUSL offers PhD (Doctor of Philosophy) by Research through the faculties of Management and Commerce (FMC), Arts and Culture (FAC), Applied Sciences (FAS), and the Biosystems Technology department of the Faculty of Technology (FT). Eligibility generally requires a good undergraduate CGPA and relevant research experience.

question: I failed a course. What are my options?
ground_truth: If you receive a grade of E (Fail, below approximately 40% of total marks), you must repeat the course in a subsequent semester. A minimum CGPA of 2.0 must be maintained to progress to the next year. If CGPA falls below 2.0 for two consecutive semesters, you may be required to withdraw from the program. Contact your Academic Advisor or HoD for guidance.

question: I got sick before my exam. What should I do?
ground_truth: Get a medical certificate from a government hospital. Submit the medical certificate to the Registrar/Examinations Division within 5 working days of the missed exam. Apply for a medical repeat examination. Approval is at the discretion of the Faculty Examination Board. Private clinic certificates may not be accepted — always use a government hospital.

question: I want to apply for the Mahapola scholarship. When and where do I apply?
ground_truth: Apply through your faculty's student affairs office during the first month of registration. The Mahapola scholarship provides approximately LKR 5,000–7,500 per month to eligible undergraduates. Eligibility is based on academic merit and financial need.

question: What is the passing mark at SEUSL?
ground_truth: The minimum passing grade is D (approximately 40% of total marks). A grade below D is classified as E (Fail). For some postgraduate or specialized courses, C (approximately 50%) may be the minimum pass. Check the specific course outline for details.

question: How far is SEUSL from Kalmunai?
ground_truth: The nearest significant town to SEUSL is Kalmunai, approximately 5 km north of the campus.

question: Which faculty is located at Sammanthurai?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Applied Sciences (FAS) is located at Sammanthurai. It was established there in 1997. The FAS contact is Phone +94 67 2260714 | Fax +94 67 2260932.

question: Who is the Head of the Department of Islamic Studies?
ground_truth: The Head of the Department of Islamic Studies is Prof. SMM. Nafees, Senior Lecturer in Islamic Law.

question: Who is the Head of the Department of Arabic Language?
ground_truth: The Head of the Department of Arabic Language is Dr. AM. Razick, Senior Lecturer in Arabic.

question: What is the FIA unique about among Sri Lankan universities?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Islamic Studies and Arabic Language (FIA) is the ONLY faculty of its kind in any university in Sri Lanka. It was established in October 2005.

question: How do I book a guest house at SEUSL?
ground_truth: Use Form GA/G/01 (Guest House Booking) available for download at https://www.seu.ac.lk/download.php. You can book lodging at university guest houses in Oluvil, Sammanthurai, or APC Mt. Lavinia, specifying A/C or Non-A/C rooms.

question: What is the institutional repository of SEUSL?
ground_truth: SEUSL's Institutional Repository (SEUIR) was established in 2006 and is available at http://ir.lib.seu.ac.lk/. It runs on DSpace 5.5 (upgraded in November 2015). It collects and provides open access to theses, dissertations, conference proceedings, journals, and research articles authored by SEUSL academics.

question: What conferences has SEUSL hosted recently?
ground_truth: SEUSL has hosted the Annual Science Research Session (ASRS) 2025, the First International Conference on AI & Data Science (ICAIDS 2025), the Sixth Annual International Research Conference by FMC, and the 11th International Symposium (INTSYMFIA 2026) by FIA.

question: What is the AHEAD project at SEUSL?
ground_truth: AHEAD stands for Accelerating Higher Education Expansion and Development. It is a funded project with activities at SEUSL aimed at improving quality and relevance of higher education. Website: http://www.seu.ac.lk/ahead/

question: ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ South Eastern University කොහේද?
ground_truth: SEUSL is located at University Park, Oluvil, #32360, Sri Lanka. (Note: question was in Sinhala asking about the location of South Eastern University.)

question: What is SEU?
ground_truth: SEU stands for South Eastern University of Sri Lanka (SEUSL). It is a national state university located in Oluvil, Sri Lanka, established in 1996. The official website is https://www.seu.ac.lk/

question: What does FAS stand for at SEUSL?
ground_truth: FAS stands for Faculty of Applied Sciences at SEUSL. It is located at Sammanthurai and offers B.Sc. programs in Biological Sciences, Chemical Sciences, Computer Science, Mathematical Sciences, and Physical Sciences.

question: What is the full form of VLE?
ground_truth: VLE stands for Virtual Learning Environment. At SEUSL, the main VLE is accessible at http://vle.seu.ac.lk/ and is used for online course delivery, assignments, and learning materials.

question: What does CGU stand for?
ground_truth: CGU stands for Career Guidance Unit at SEUSL. It was established in 1998 and assists students with career planning, job placement, internships, CV writing, and interview preparation. Director: Dr. J.A. Thamboo.

question: How do I contact the Faculty of Technology?
ground_truth: Faculty of Technology contact: Dean Dr. U.L. Abdul Majeed, Email: ulmajeed@seu.ac.lk, Phone: +94 67 2052818, Mobile: +94 77 3121350. Faculty website: www.seu.ac.lk/ft/ and contact page: http://www.seu.ac.lk/ft/contact.php

question: Which faculty dean can be contacted at munas@seu.ac.lk?
ground_truth: The email munas@seu.ac.lk belongs to Ash Sheikh. MHA. Munas, the Dean of the Faculty of Islamic Studies and Arabic Language (FIA). His mobile number is +94 77 2226131.

question: I want to study biology at SEUSL. Who is my HoD?
ground_truth: If you study biology (B.Sc. in Biological Sciences), your Head of Department is Mr. AM Riyas Ahamed at the Department of Biological Sciences, Faculty of Applied Sciences. Email: riyasahame@yahoo.co.uk, Contact: +94 776009200.

question: Which faculty offers the B.Sc. in Biosystems Technology?
ground_truth: The B.Sc. in Biosystems Technology is offered by the Department of Biosystems Technology (BST) within the Faculty of Technology (FT). It focuses on agricultural technology, bioprocessing, environmental technology, and food technology.

question: Who directs the SSSW unit?
ground_truth: The Student Support Services and Welfare (SSSW) unit is directed by Dr. ALM. Ayoobkhan (Senior Lecturer in E-Commerce). Email: directorsssw@seu.ac.lk / ayoob@seu.ac.lk. Phone: +94 67 2255389, Mobile: +94 77 8684242.

question: Does SEUSL offer a degree in medicine or law?
ground_truth: No, SEUSL does not currently offer degree programs in Medicine or Law. The six faculties are Arts and Culture, Management and Commerce, Applied Sciences, Islamic Studies and Arabic Language, Engineering, and Technology. For medicine or law, students should look at other Sri Lankan national universities.

question: Can I get a degree from SEUSL online or through distance learning?
ground_truth: SEUSL primarily offers on-campus programs. The Centre for Extension and Distance and Professional Learning (CEDPL) offers some extension education and distance learning programs. The VLE supports online learning components. For full distance degree programs, contact CEDPL at http://www.seu.ac.lk/cedpl/index.php for current offerings.

question: Does SEUSL have a dentistry faculty?
ground_truth: No, SEUSL does not have a Faculty of Dentistry. SEUSL has six faculties: Arts and Culture, Management and Commerce, Applied Sciences, Islamic Studies and Arabic Language, Engineering, and Technology.

question: What is the Peradeniya University ranking?
ground_truth: This question is about the University of Peradeniya, not SEUSL. The SEUSL SmartBot can only provide information about South Eastern University of Sri Lanka (SEUSL). For information about other universities, please visit their respective websites or the UGC website at http://www.ugc.ac.lk/

question: Is there a fee to apply to SEUSL as an undergraduate?
ground_truth: Undergraduate admissions to SEUSL are processed through the University Grants Commission (UGC) of Sri Lanka. There are no tuition fees for UGC-admitted undergraduate students. However, once enrolled, students pay small administrative fees (registration, examination, library, hostel). Application for admissions is handled by UGC — check http://www.ugc.ac.lk/ for any UGC application fees.

question: Which dean can be contacted at +94 67 2255182?
ground_truth: The phone number +94 67 2255182 belongs to Prof. (Dr.) MM. Fazil, the Dean of the Faculty of Arts and Culture (FAC). His email is fazrasm@seu.ac.lk.

question: Is the Faculty of Applied Sciences at the main Oluvil campus?
ground_truth: No. The Faculty of Applied Sciences (FAS) is located at Sammanthurai, not the main Oluvil campus. It was established there in 1997. FAS contact: Phone +94 67 2260714 | Fax +94 67 2260932.

question: What forms do I need to request a repair for my computer at SEUSL?
ground_truth: Use the ERSU Repair Request form (Electronics Repair Service Unit), available for download at https://www.seu.ac.lk/download.php. This form covers repair/service of CPU, UPS, monitors, printers, laptops, CCTV, photocopiers, and other electronic equipment.

question: Both BST and ICT are in the Faculty of Technology. What is the difference between their postgraduate offerings?
ground_truth: The BST department of the Faculty of Technology offers MPhil and PhD by Research in Biosystems Technology. The ICT department of the Faculty of Technology does not have listed postgraduate research programs — it offers Short-Term Training Programmes (STTP) open to external candidates. MPhil/PhD in Biosystems Technology applicants should contact the Postgraduate Unit (PGUFT).

question: I need a student confirmation letter for my bank. How do I get it?
ground_truth: Submit a written request or online form to the Registrar's office specifying the purpose (bank account). The letter is usually issued within 3–5 working days. A small fee may apply. Bring your student ID when collecting it. Contact: registrar@seu.ac.lk or +94 67 2255168.

question: What are the available short-term programs for external candidates at SEUSL?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Technology's ICT Department offers Short-Term Training Programmes (STTP) open to external candidates. Applications can be submitted at https://www.seu.ac.lk/ft/stc.php. The Faculty of Applied Sciences also offers the Diploma in Computer Technology (DCT) and Postgraduate Diploma in Applied Statistics (PGDAS) for external applicants.

question: Who is the Head of DELT at SEUSL?
ground_truth: DELT stands for Department of English Language Teaching, which is part of the Faculty of Arts and Culture. The Head of DELT is Dr. MIF. Kareema, Senior Lecturer in English.

question: What language does the Faculty of Islamic Studies use for teaching?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Islamic Studies and Arabic Language (FIA) uses Arabic as the primary medium of instruction.

question: How can I access the SEUSL VLE from home?
ground_truth: The main VLE is accessible at http://vle.seu.ac.lk/ from any internet connection. The FAS VLE is at https://fasvle.seu.ac.lk/. Log in with your student index number and university credentials. Taylor & Francis eBooks require Shibboleth/Remote Access for off-campus use. Contact the ICT Centre for login support.

question: Is there a mosque on the SEUSL campus?
ground_truth: Yes. Given the predominantly Muslim environment of SEUSL and Oluvil, mosque facilities and prayer rooms are available on and near campus. Prayer times are respected in the academic schedule. Non-Muslim students are welcomed and treated with full respect.

question: What is the nearest hospital to SEUSL?
ground_truth: The nearest hospital is Kalmunai Base Hospital, approximately 5 km from campus. The university health centre handles minor ailments and refers serious conditions to Kalmunai Base Hospital.

question: Who are the Heads of Department for all five departments in FAS?
ground_truth: The Heads of Department for all five FAS departments are: (1) Biological Sciences: Mr. AM Riyas Ahamed (riyasahame@yahoo.co.uk, +94 776009200); (2) Chemical Sciences: Mr. MF Nawas (mfnawas@seu.ac.lk, +94 777900946); (3) Computer Science: Mr. AL Hanees (alhanees@seu.ac.lk, +94 760123729); (4) Mathematical Sciences: Dr. M.C. Alibuhtto (mcabuhtto@seu.ac.lk, +94 776201677); (5) Physical Sciences: Dr. T. Jaseetharan (jaseetharan@seu.ac.lk, +94 772852827).

question: What is the library called and when is it open?
ground_truth: The main SEUSL library is called the Dr. A.M.A. Azeez Library. It is open Monday to Friday 8:00 AM–8:00 PM and Saturday 8:00 AM–5:00 PM. It is closed on Sundays and public holidays. Extended hours may be available before exams.

question: I want to submit my research article to the university repository. What are the rules?
ground_truth: To submit to the SEUIR (http://ir.lib.seu.ac.lk/), only SEUSL members may submit, and only the SEUIR administrator or authorized persons can deposit content. Academics can upload to the 'Research Articles' collection. You are fully responsible for content validity. If you do not own copyright, obtain publisher permission first. Research articles from predatory journals or conferences are excluded. Submit via the authorized SEUIR administrator.

question: Which faculty is Prof. S. M. Junaideen originally from?
ground_truth: Prof. S. M. Junaideen, the current Vice Chancellor of SEUSL, is originally from the Faculty of Engineering (FE). His profile is at http://fe.seu.ac.lk/staff/academic/index.php?fun=FE0_001
question: What is the AHEAD project at SEUSL?
ground_truth: AHEAD stands for Accelerating Higher Education Expansion and Development. It is a funded project at SEUSL. Website: http://www.seu.ac.lk/ahead/.

question: Where can I find the Faculty of Applied Sciences VLE?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Applied Sciences Virtual Learning Environment (VLE) is available at https://fasvle.seu.ac.lk/.

question: What is the Faculty of Technology's website?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Technology's website is www.seu.ac.lk/ft/ and can be contacted at +94 67 2052818.

question: What is the Faculty of Management and Commerce's contact number?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Management and Commerce can be contacted at Phone: +94 67 2255386, Fax: +94 67 2255069.

question: What is the FIA contact number?
ground_truth: The Faculty of Islamic Studies and Arabic Language (FIA) can be contacted at Phone: +94 67 2255127, Fax: +94 67 2255385.

question: Does SEUSL offer Short-Term Training Programmes?
ground_truth: Yes, the Department of ICT in the Faculty of Technology offers Short-Term Training Programmes (STTP) for external candidates. Application: https://www.seu.ac.lk/ft/stc.php.

question: What is the SEUSL Physical Education Unit?
ground_truth: The Physical Education Unit manages sports facilities, university sports teams, and physical education programs at SEUSL. Website: http://www.seu.ac.lk/physicaleducationunit/index.php.

question: What is the 11th International Symposium (INTSYMFIA 2026)?
ground_truth: The 11th International Symposium (INTSYMFIA 2026) is hosted by the Faculty of Islamic Studies and Arabic Language (FIA) at SEUSL. It is an international academic event with a call for articles/papers.
